findings.description: u0001 defines a distinct title-case rule for headlines/headings (with example) that is not represented by any candidate rule. The group only covers body copy, campaign headline, and the negative ALL CAPS rule, leaving the headline title-case standard unextracted.

payload.example: LISRAYA Targets the Root Cause of Dermatomyositis
payload.text: Headlines/headings use title case (initial caps): capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, adverbs; lowercase articles, conjunctions, prepositions.

findings.description: u0003 (chart/infographic headlines: ALL CAPS, set in Agenda/Nunito Sans Bold+) is a distinct rule with its own typeface/scope specification and is not captured by any candidate rule; it is also a named exception referenced implicitly by the negative ALL CAPS rule (u0005).

payload.text: Chart/infographic headlines use ALL CAPS, set in Agenda (print) or Nunito Sans Bold+ (email fallback).

findings.description: The negative rule ('other than the cases above') is an exception-governed rule whose exceptions (campaign headline ALL CAPS, chart/infographic headline ALL CAPS) are not linked as related rules, losing the exception relationship encoded in the source.
Source cites a cross-reference '(see 1.6)' indicating additional governing detail elsewhere in the bible; this external relation/citation is not captured on the rule.

The [GENERAL] tag signals this rule governs broadly across content types (body, buttons, subheads) but the candidate rule lacks an explicit scope/applicability facet enumerating these targets, weakening enforceability tracking.
